summary: The diff modifies TransactionImport.tsx and pdfParser.ts, but critically, pdfParser.ts still uses CDN-based dynamic loading (PDFJS_CDN and PDFJS_WORKER_CDN constants pointing to cdnjs.cloudflare.com) rather than replacing it with the bundled pdfjs-dist npm package. The core requirement of REQ-8 — replacing CDN-based dynamic import with a bundled npm package — is not implemented. The zero-transaction error message and TransactionImport routing improvements appear to be present.

missing_requirements.requirement: Replace the CDN-based dynamic import with a bundled pdfjs-dist npm package so that PDF parsing works reliably client-side without network dependency.
missing_requirements.reason: The modified pdfParser.ts still contains PDFJS_CDN and PDFJS_WORKER_CDN constants pointing to cdnjs.cloudflare.com and dynamically injects a script tag to load PDF.js from CDN. There is no import from pdfjs-dist and no evidence of pdfjs-dist being installed as an npm dependency.

missing_requirements.requirement: Configure the PDF.js worker correctly using a local worker URL or disabling the worker to avoid worker initialization errors.
missing_requirements.reason: The worker is still configured via PDFJS_WORKER_CDN pointing to a remote CDN URL, not a local bundled worker. No local worker configuration or worker disabling is present.
